services.name: dotcms
services.description: Hybrid headless content management system for building modern websites, apps and digital experiences with a Java-based, API-first platform.
services.installation:
Default admin login is `admin@dotcms.com` / `admin`. Change DOT_INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD before exposing the instance.

Provisions two addons automatically:
- PostgreSQL 16 via the CloudNativePG operator (`dotcms-db`) â€” dotCMS supports PostgreSQL 13-16. Requires the CloudNativePG operator on the cluster.
- OpenSearch 1.3.x (`dotcms-opensearch`) â€” dotCMS does not support OpenSearch 2.x or 3.x. Do not bump the image tag.

This is the single-instance tier. For a clustered, failover-capable deployment (3-node Postgres + 2 dotCMS replicas) use the dotCMS (HA) template.

First boot takes 2-5 minutes while dotCMS initializes the schema and indices. Wait for both addons to become Ready before the dotCMS pod will pass its startup checks.

services.name: humhub
services.description: Flexible open-source social network kit for private communities and team collaboration.
services.installation: Set HUMHUB_HOST to your public hostname. Set HUMHUB_ADMIN_PASSWORD before first boot for auto-install.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: easyappointments
services.description: Open-source appointment scheduler for service providers — booking pages, calendars, and notifications.
services.installation: Set BASE_URL to your public URL before first visit. Complete admin setup on first login.

services.name: password-pusher
services.description: Securely share passwords and files with expiring links — open-source alternative to one-time secret services.
services.installation: Generate PWPUSH_MASTER_KEY before production. Set TLS_DOMAIN for automatic HTTPS if supported.

services.name: zulip
services.description: Powerful open-source team chat organized into topic-based threads — an alternative to Slack.
services.installation: Set SETTING_EXTERNAL_HOST and SECRETS_secret_key before first boot. Initial startup can take 5–10 minutes while Zulip configures all services.
